evidence_excerpt: Initial PET-CT showed no distant metastases, but later PET/CT demonstrated new metastatic right lower lobe pulmonary lesions and nodal disease; bronchoscopy found the bronchus intermedius 100% occluded by tumor requiring endobronchial debulking.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing management needs (fentanyl patch for pain, bowel regimen, chronic Foley, and antibiotics for post-obstructive pneumonia) but he denied major respiratory or systemic symptoms at discharge.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: PET/CT showed metastatic disease in the right lower lobe pulmonary lesions and nodal involvement after an earlier PET-CT had no distant metastases.
Admitted for concern of obstruction (no ostomy output for 3 days with emesis) and for a planned bronchoscopy.
Rigid and flexible bronchoscopy with tumor destruction/debridement (laser, electrocautery, cryodebridement), EBUS TBNA, and fiducial placement for airway obstruction.
Post-obstructive pneumonia suspected with purulent airway secretions noted; treated with amoxicillin-clavulanate.
Ostomy output improved with magnesium citrate and milk of magnesia along with his usual bowel regimen.
After bronchoscopy he was briefly 85% on 4L but improved to 96% on room air a few hours later.

notes_on_uncertainty: Dates are redacted, so events are placed relative to the established diagnosis and initial treatment. Long-term follow-up beyond the discharge is not documented; functional status and symptom burden reflect the status at the time of this later hospitalization and discharge. Time periods are marked unknown due to insufficient date information.
